summary: Frontend-only visual/integrity test of the new CortexViral dark dashboard theme. Tested 10 dashboard pages + landing page via Playwright with session_token cookie auth for the admin test user. Confirmed dark theme applied via DashboardLayout + .cv-dash-scope CSS overrides — every page has bg=rgb(9,9,11), ZERO white card backgrounds found inside scope, sidebar wordmark/logo/admin-pill/logout testids all present, 18 nav items rendered. Landing page unaffected. No React/runtime errors thrown.
critical_code_review_comments: DashboardLayout sidebar has a small a11y concern: the logo button is a <button> but acts like a link to /. Consider <a href='/'> for proper semantics + middle-click open.

CSS override uses !important pervasively — that's intentional and acceptable here, but any future component that needs a true white surface inside the dashboard will need to opt-out by using a different class than bg-white.

The 401 console errors on dashboard mount (from one or two backend endpoints) are not theme-related but suggest a stale or restricted session for certain endpoints — worth a separate look; pages still render fine.
test_credentials: Used session cookie session_token=test_session_1779636592168 from /app/memory/test_credentials.md (test@automatex.dev, is_admin=true).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Dashboard theme overhaul is visually solid: .cv-dash-scope in index.css successfully reskins bg-white, neutral-* text/borders, pastel KPI bgs, input/textarea/combobox to dark glass without per-page edits. If new dashboard pages are added later that use Tailwind classes NOT in the override block (e.g., bg-slate-50, bg-gray-100, raw shadcn Popover content with bg-popover), they may render light and need new entries in the .cv-dash-scope block. The 401/ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED console errors during dashboard load are pre-existing backend/network noise — not theme-related — and did not crash any page.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found in the requested scope.
